title: Depth Recurrence via Layer Sharing (3 shared blocks → 1/3 params, matched BPB)
author: SkywardSyntax

data.description: Uses 3 unique transformer blocks cycled over multiple virtual layers with ALBERT-style weight sharing, preserving forward depth while reducing unique parameters.
Shares transformer block weights across repeated virtual layers.
Preserves encoder/decoder skip connections and x0 residual blending across shared blocks to prevent representation collapse.

other_params.shared_params_handling: gradients accumulate correctly across repeated applications

novel_contributions: Cycles 3 unique transformer blocks over 9 virtual layers to achieve depth recurrence with 1/3 the unique parameters.
Matches baseline BPB while reducing artifact size substantially.
Enables extra recurrence cycles at evaluation time for additional BPB gains.
Minimal code change via a NUM_UNIQUE_LAYERS environment variable.
Demonstrates compatibility with shared-parameter optimization using Muon + Adam.
